meaning: n.裂缝,裂沟,(思想,观点等的)分歧v.(使)裂开,(使)分裂
examples: The image of the inferior oblique fissure can be showed by the CT scan.
A 25-kilometre-long fissure erupted 12 km3 of lava over the course of seven months.
The double-life I'd be leading was shattered, a permanent fissure finally ripped through.
In order to speak, Ms. T. had to cover her neck fissure with her hand.
He added that it illustrates there is "a huge fissure opening up in the Tory party".
